channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Japan Scraps Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Historic Defense Shift
item.description: Japan has overturned decades of postwar pacifist policy by lifting restrictions on overseas arms sales, opening the door for exports of warships, missiles and other weapons as it seeks to counter regional security threats.

item.title: UN Secretary-General Candidates Face Live Public Grillings in New York
item.description: Four candidates vying to become the next UN secretary-general face televised hearings Tuesday and Wednesday, marking a rare public scrutiny process for the leadership of the crisis-wracked global organization.

item.title: US Pushes for Iran Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: Security remains tight in Pakistan ahead of hoped-for talks between US and Iranian delegations as a two-week ceasefire set to expire Wednesday, with Vance and top officials expected to arrive in Islamabad.

item.title: Japan Overhauls Pacifist Defense Policy to Enter Global Arms Market
item.description: The Japanese government has scrapped restrictions on overseas arms sales in a major shift from post-WWII pacifist policies, seeking to counter regional security threats from China and North Korea.

item.title: Iran Demands Release of Seized Vessel as Ceasefire Deadline Nears
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and top US officials are expected to depart for Islamabad ahead of potential peace talks, while Iran files UN complaint over US seizure of cargo vessel Touska.

item.title: Iran grants field commanders greater autonomy over Iraq militias amid war pressures
item.description: Iran has given its commanders more power to direct militia operations in Iraq without central approval, a decentralization driven by the ongoing conflict with the US. This shift could lead to more unpredictable attacks and complicates diplomatic efforts.

item.title: Seized Iranian ship Touska carried dual-use equipment, US sources say
item.description: The Iranian-flagged container ship seized by US forces on Sunday was likely carrying equipment that could have both civilian and military applications. The seizure highlights continued enforcement of the US naval blockade despite ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: Trump Board of Peace in talks with DP World on Gaza reconstruction
item.description: Representatives of Trump's "Board of Peace" have held discussions with Dubai-owned DP World about rebuilding Gaza infrastructure. The talks signal early planning for post-war reconstruction even as ceasefire negotiations continue.

item.title: Weak Indian monsoon threatens volatile crop prices amid Iran crisis
item.description: A forecasted weak monsoon in India could drive agricultural price volatility at a time when global food markets are already stressed by the Iran conflict and Hormuz disruptions. The combination could hit Asian consumers particularly hard.

item.title: US military veterans arrested on Capitol Hill protesting Iran war
item.description: Dozens of US military veterans were arrested while protesting the Iran war at the Capitol. Anti-war groups say the conflict is deeply unpopular and becoming a political crisis for the Trump administration as ceasefire deadline approaches.

item.title: Trump China policy drifts as tariffs stall amid Iran war focus
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to reset relations with China through tariffs, but the Iran war has shifted priorities, leaving his China policy without clear direction.

item.title: Pakistan confident Iran will attend US peace talks
item.description: A senior Pakistani official told Reuters that Pakistan is confident it can get Iran to attend talks with the United States, as diplomatic efforts intensify before the ceasefire deadline.

item.title: Vice President Vance has not yet departed for Iran talks in Pakistan
item.description: JD Vance remains in the US as prospects for a second round of negotiations with Iran remain unclear. Iran has condemned recent US naval actions as armed piracy.

item.title: Board of Peace envoy says Gaza disarmament plan needs quick progress
item.description: The lead envoy for Trumps Board of Peace expressed cautious optimism about a Gaza disarmament plan, but cautioned Hamas talks will not be easy and time is critical.

item.title: Oil prices fall as markets bet on US-Iran peace talks proceeding
item.description: Crude oil fell over $1 per barrel on expectations that US-Iran peace talks will take place this week, potentially reopening critical Middle East supply routes.

item.title: Labor Secretary Chavez-DeRemer resigns from Trump Cabinet amid abuse allegations
item.description: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer left President Trump's Cabinet after allegations including an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job. She is the third Cabinet member to depart, following the firing of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem and Attorney General Pam Bondi.

item.title: US expresses confidence in Iran peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms
item.description: The United States expressed confidence that peace talks with Iran would proceed in Pakistan, with a senior Iranian official saying Tehran is considering joining. The fragile two-week ceasefire is set to expire amid uncertainty over whether negotiations will continue.

item.title: China's Xi calls for Strait of Hormuz to remain open in talks with Saudi crown prince
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping held a phone call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, calling for normal passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz to be maintained as Beijing steps up diplomatic efforts to help end the Iran war.

item.title: Trump denies Israel influenced his decision to attack Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump stated that Israel did not persuade him to attack Iran, pushing back against reports that Benjamin Netanyahu influenced his decision and responding to criticism from right-wing commentators.

item.title: UN secretary-general race heats up as candidates emerge to replace Guterres
item.description: The 10th United Nations secretary-general will be elected this year for a term starting January 1, 2027. Multiple candidates are positioning themselves to succeed Antonio Guterres as the race gains momentum.

item.title: Japan lifts decades-old ban on lethal arms exports in major policy shift
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, marking a significant shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

item.title: China's youth unemployment rises to 16.9% ahead of record graduation season
item.description: China's official youth jobless rate climbed to 16.9% in March, with another 12.7 million graduates set to enter the workforce, deepening the employment crunch.

item.title: China bets on coal conversion to secure energy amid Iran war disruption
item.description: Far from its eastern metropolises, China is doubling down on coal as a core element of its energy and industrial security strategy, seeking to reduce vulnerability to supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Three ships including Iranian vessel attempt Hormuz transits
item.description: Three vessels, including one Iranian-flagged ship and two others with unclear ownership, attempted to transit the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions and blockade concerns.

item.title: Major Indonesian natural gas discovery announced
item.description: A significant natural gas find in Indonesia could unlock substantial new volumes for both domestic consumption and international export markets.
